digest.component_or_area: MCP tool reference generator + CI drift gate
propositions: Generate docs/mcp-reference.md from the MCP server's own registrars, classified by gate tier, mirroring the gen_env_registry.py generator + drift-gate pattern.
The generator must enumerate each registrar with REBAR_MCP_READONLY=0 so write tools register, since a naive single list_tools() in ambient env produces a partial reference.

Tools are classified by registrar default gate plus a closed four-entry override table (reconcile, fsck, sign_review, run_workflow) for hybrid cases, with a drift check asserting these names stay in their expected registrars.

The reference emits exactly three labeled gate-tier sections (Read-only, LLM-gated, Write-gated) with the four special cases as inline annotations rather than separate sections; sign_review belongs in Write-gated not LLM-gated.
A --check drift mode fails on a stale committed file and must be wired into both test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml, enforced by the CI workflow parity test.
Acceptance requires all 47 tools listed (23 read + 6 llm + 18 write) with deterministic section-bounded assertions verifying special-case placement.
